articles.title: Trump Was Flattering, Xi Was Resolute. The Difference Spoke Volumes.
articles.summary: In contrast to his rhetoric about China at home, President Trump spoke in conciliatory terms with Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader.

articles.title: As Trump meets with Xi, security expert says China now faces the U.S. as a peer
articles.summary: Former national security official Rush Doshi says President Trump's sky-high tariffs on Chinese goods sparked a clash in which China prevailed. We look at the current state of U.S.-China relations.

articles.title: Spies, Sanctions, Cyberattacks: China and the U.S. Clash Behind the Scenes
articles.summary: After months of avoiding confrontation, the Trump administration has taken recent steps to call out China on Iran, artificial intelligence and spying.

articles.title: Trump Might Welcome Chinese Investment, but America Is Wary
articles.summary: A pledge for more Chinese investment could face backlash given longstanding national security concerns in the United States.

articles.title: An LA-area mayor acted as an agent for China. Experts say it's part of a pattern
articles.summary: Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.

articles.title: Trump tries to make deals in China. And, Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed leader
articles.summary: President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.

articles.title: Cordial Trump-Xi meeting is a 'good sign,' says longtime U.S. diplomat
articles.summary: Longtime U.S. diplomat Richard Haass interprets President Trump's meeting with China leader, Xi Jinping. He said though the two have different priorities the cordial summit could be a 'good sign.'

articles.title: China's leader warns Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to a clash
articles.summary: At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.

articles.title: President Trump's first day in China
articles.summary: President Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jingping {shee-jihn-ping} during his first day in the country. A look at what happened at the high-stakes summit.

articles.title: Jury orders Boeing to pay $49.5 million to family of 737 MAX crash victim
articles.summary: A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.

articles.title: U.S. Set to Drop Charges Against Indian Billionaire Accused of Fraud
articles.summary: The decision came after a meeting in which a lawyer for the billionaire, Gautam Adani, made an unusual offer, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Inside Jack Schlossberg’s Chaotic Campaign to Revive Camelot
articles.summary: Erratic behavior and staff turnover have colored Mr. Schlossberg’s bid for a House seat in New York, raising questions about his readiness for office.

articles.title: Strategist Tied to Becerra and Newsom Pleads Guilty in Corruption Case
articles.summary: Dana Williamson, a former political strategist for Xavier Becerra and a former aide to Gov. Gavin Newsom, appeared in federal court on Thursday.

articles.title: Researchers unearth Southeast Asia's largest dinosaur
articles.summary: They're calling it the "last titan" of Thailand. The sauropod — an herbivore with a long neck and tail — comes from the late Early Cretaceous period, some 100 to 120 million years ago.

articles.title: A Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
articles.summary: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.

articles.title: In a City of Big Dreams, Many Young Adults See a Cloudy Future
articles.summary: A bleak job market. Rising rents. Huge debt. In New York and other cities, traditional milestones of adulthood feel further away for some 20- and 30-year-olds.

articles.title: The New Yorker's Evan Osnos shares his thoughts on the Trump-Xi summit
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep talks with the New Yorker's Evan Osnos about his impressions of the Trump-Xi summit.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon meet in D.C. again for peace talks
articles.summary: Representatives for Israel and Lebanon meet in Washington, D.C., for a second time to end the war, as their ceasefire frays.

articles.title: Why the Migrant Child Crisis Is Roiling the California Governor Race
articles.summary: The Times broke the story that has become a dominant line of attack against Xavier Becerra, the Democratic front-runner. Here are five things to know about it.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
